differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis secondary to remote abdominal trauma
Congestive hepatopathy due to occult cardiac disease
next_steps: Perform MR angiography (MRA) of the abdomen to better characterize portal venous anatomy and collateral circulation
Perform a percutaneous liver biopsy with special staining for Schistosoma ova
Echocardiogram to rule out right-sided heart failure or constrictive pericarditis
Monitor hemodynamic stability and address abdominal pain with conservative management
Consult interventional radiology for potential evaluation of portal venous patency

differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis with portal cavernoma and portal biliopathy
next_steps: Perform CT angiography of the abdomen to evaluate for mesenteric artery stenosis or compression (median arcuate ligament syndrome) to investigate postprandial pain
Consult interventional radiology for transjugular liver biopsy with hepatic venous pressure gradient (HVPG) measurement
Order serologic testing for Schistosoma species
Obtain an echocardiogram to rule out underlying constrictive pericarditis or restrictive cardiomyopathy
Perform MR cholangiopancreatography (MRCP) to assess for portal biliopathy
Refer for nutrition consultation to address significant weight loss and optimize intake
